title: A Valediction
content:
If we must part,
    Then let it be like this;
    Not heart on heart,
    Nor with the useless anguish of a kiss;
    But touch mine hand and say:
    "Until to-morrow or some other day,
    If we must part."

    Words are so weak
    When love hath been so strong:
    Let silence speak:
    "Life is a little while, and love is long;
    A time to sow and reap,
    And after harvest a long time to sleep.
    But words are weak."
meaning:
Ernest Christopher Dowson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Valediction"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
